Alginate powders are commonly used in dental impressions where their gelling ability upon contact with water creates a mold-like structure useful for creating casts of teeth and mouth structures. The key information here relates to how magnesium oxide (mgo) interacts with alginate powder. Alginate powders are commonly used in dental impressions where their gelling ability upon contact with water creates a mold-like structure useful for creating casts of teeth and mouth structures. Adding mgo could potentially alter one of the physical characteristics of the alginate mixture significantly impacting its performance during impression making procedures.
